creator: lmayer@axigenmail.com
text:
Updated patch attached with more changes in "Resouces and Links" section. Please apply new patch to projects/c99/index.sgml revision 1.113 New patch: --- c99_1113.sgml 2008-04-23 10:20:03.423737600 -0700 +++ c99_1114.sgml 2008-04-24 10:03:48.749323200 -0700 @@ -48,7 +48,7 @@

The FreeBSD C99 & &posix; Conformance Project aims to implement all requirements of the ISO 9899:1999 (C99) and - IEEE 1003.1-2001 (POSIX) standards. In cases where aspects of + IEEE 1003.1-2004 (POSIX) standards. In cases where aspects of these standards cannot be followed, those aspects will be documented in the c99(7) or posix(7) manuals. It is also an aim of this project to implement regression tests to ensure correctness @@ -78,26 +78,26 @@ alt="Resources and Links">

    -

  • ISO/IEC 9899:1999, also known as C99, is available +

  • INCITS/ISO/IEC 9899-1999 (R2005), also known as C99, is available for purchase in electronic form from the - American - National Standards Institute, or available from the - International - Organization for Standardization store in electronic - and paper form.

    • -

  • IEEE Std 1003.1-2001 - - The free (registration required), online version of - POSIX.1-2001 and SUSv3 standard.

    • + American + National Standards Institute.

  • Are you Ready For C99? - This article discusses some of the new features in C99.

    +

  • IEEE Std 1003.1-2004, also known as POSIX.1-2004 and SUSv3, can be + viewed online (free registration required) or + + downloaded from IEEE Xplore (available at university libraries).

    • +

  • ANSI/IEEE Std 1003.3-1991 (ISO/IEC13210: 1994 (E)), Test methods for measuring conformance to POSIX, can be + + downloaded from IEEE Xplore (available at university libraries).

    • +

  • Jens Schweikhardt maintains a + list + with the current status of POSIX Utility Conformance in FreeBSD.

  • Most discussion relating to this project takes place on the &a.standards; mailing list. To subscribe to the list, send a message to &a.standards.subscribe;.

    • -

  • Jens Schweikhardt maintains a - list - with the current status of POSIX Utility Conformance in - FreeBSD.
